sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_table (id UInt64, value String, array_col Array(UInt64) DEFAULT emptyArrayUInt64());
INSERT INTO test_table (id, value, array_col) VALUES (1, 'example', emptyArrayUInt64()), (2, 'test', emptyArrayUInt64());
SELECT id, value, multiIf(array_col = emptyArrayUInt64(), 'empty', 'not empty') AS array_status FROM test_table GROUP BY id, value HAVING array_status = 'empty';
SELECT id, value, arrayJoin(array_col) AS expanded FROM test_table WHERE multiIf(length(value) > 5, true, false) AND array_col = emptyArrayUInt64();
SELECT id, count() AS count_val, multiIf(array_col = emptyArrayUInt64(), 'no data', 'data present') AS data_status FROM test_table GROUP BY data_status;

SELECT id, value FROM test_table WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM (SELECT id, aggregateFunction(count, id) AS cnt FROM test_table GROUP BY id HAVING cnt > 1)) AND multiIf(array_col = emptyArrayUInt64(), 1, 0) = 1;
CREATE TABLE test_table2 (id UInt64, value Nullable(String), array_col Array(UInt64) DEFAULT emptyArrayUInt64());
INSERT INTO test_table2 (id, value, array_col) VALUES (1, NULL, emptyArrayUInt64()), (2, 'example', emptyArrayUInt64());
SELECT id, count() AS non_null_count, multiIf(value IS NULL, 'NULL', 'NON-NULL') AS value_status FROM test_table2 GROUP BY value_status HAVING non_null_count > 0;
SELECT t1.id, t1.value, t2.array_col FROM test_table t1 JOIN test_table2 t2 ON multiIf(t1.id = t2.id, true, false) WHERE t2.array_col = emptyArrayUInt64();
